\section{Introduction}
\label{sec:intro}

We propose a novel method. We design a new architecture. We implement the system.
We evaluate the performance. We compare with baselines.

This is a very long sentence that contains many words and clauses and subclauses and keeps going on and on without any clear point being made which makes it extremely difficult for the reader to follow the argument being presented in this particular paragraph of the paper.

\section{Background}
\label{sec:bg}

Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) enables computation on encrypted data.
The key operation is the Number Theoretic Transform (NTT).

\section{Method}
\label{sec:method}

Our approach consists of three components.
First, we optimize the NTT butterfly unit.
Second, we pipeline the key-switching operation.
Third, we introduce a novel memory hierarchy.
